markets.question: New Rihanna Album before GTA VI?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Rihanna officially releases a new album before Grand Theft Auto VI is officially released in the US. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

If neither occurs by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to 50-50.

For the purposes of this market, "release" refers to the game becoming publicly available for purchase or download in the US. Early access, beta versions, other forms of pre-release availability, or leaks will not count as an official release. If the release is only for certain consoles (e.g. Xbox Series X/S) it will count.

The resolution source for the release of GTA VI is official information from Rockstar Games or its parent company, Take-Two Interactive.

Officially released means that a newly released album is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date.

Re-releases, deluxe editions, compilations, or greatest hits albums will not count, unless they contain at least 50% tracks that have never been officially released in any format prior to the album's release.

The resolution source for the album release will be any official streaming or download site, e.g. Apple Music or Spotify.

events.title: What will happen before GTA VI?
events.description: This is a market on predicting events that will occur before the release of Grand Theft Auto VI.

eventMetadata.context_description: Rockstar Games' repeated delays have extended GTA VI's release to November 19, 2026, giving traders a defined window for events like major tech launches, album drops, and geopolitical shifts to resolve before launch. Recent Take-Two earnings calls confirmed ongoing marketing plans and physical editions while underscoring the title's massive cultural weight, which amplifies trader focus on high-visibility outcomes such as GPT-6 availability or new music from artists like Playboi Carti and Rihanna. Industry dynamics favor betting on near-term cultural milestones, as GTA VI's dominance in gaming discourse creates strong incentives for accurate assessment of what will capture public attention first. Key upcoming catalysts include any summer 2026 trailer updates or platform announcements that could shift momentum ahead of the November lock.

markets.question: Will Harvey Weinstein be sentenced to no prison time?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the prison sentence, if any, imposed on Harvey Weinstein as part of his ongoing New York retrial (People of the State of New York v. Harvey Weinstein) by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

This market will resolve immediately based on the first sentence rendered in this case, regardless of any appeals. If Weinstein is found not guilty, there is a mistrial, or if the first sentencing does not include any jail or prison time, this market will resolve to "No Prison Time."

If no sentencing takes place by July 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will also resolve to "No Prison Time."

If the sentence falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

For the purposes of this market, it does not matter whether the sentence is concurrent or consecutive to any existing sentence Weinstein is already serving. The market will resolve based on the total prison sentence imposed in this case.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the New York court system or other involved U.S. government sources; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: The recent mistrial in Harvey Weinstein's third New York rape trial on May 15, 2026, stands as the dominant factor behind the market's heavy weighting toward no prison time. After a deadlocked jury failed to reach a verdict on the remaining charge involving Jessica Mann, prosecutors now have 30 days to decide whether to pursue a fourth trial on the same allegation. This outcome follows mixed results from prior proceedings, including a 2025 New York conviction on one count and Weinstein's separate 16-year California sentence for rape and sexual assault. At age 74 and with documented health concerns, the disgraced producer faces significant barriers to additional lengthy incarceration, even as existing convictions keep him behind bars for now. Upcoming decisions on retrial and sentencing will likely shape any shifts in trader sentiment.

markets.question: Will the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Carolina Hurricanes win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

This market will resolve to “No” if it becomes impossible for this team to win the 2026 NHL Stanley Cup based off the rules of the NHL.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the NHL.

events.title: 2026 NHL Stanley Cup Champion
events.description: This market is to predict the winner of the 2025–26 NHL Stanley Cup championship.

eventMetadata.context_description: The 2026 Stanley Cup Final between the Carolina Hurricanes and Vegas Golden Knights drives current trader consensus, with the series tied 1-1 after split results. Carolina captured Game 2 in overtime on Seth Jarvis’s power-play goal to even the series following Vegas’s Game 1 road win, keeping implied probabilities close. The Hurricanes finished the regular season with the Eastern Conference’s top record at 113 points and have shown strong playoff form, while the Golden Knights advanced with consistent depth and recent momentum. Game 3 in Las Vegas features near-even odds, with injury uncertainty around Vegas defenseman Brayden McNabb adding to the matchup’s balance.

If at any point it becomes impossible for this team to win the FIFA World Cup based on the rules of FIFA (e.g., they are eliminated in the knockout stage), this market will resolve immediately to “No”.

If the 2026 FIFA World Cup is permanently canceled or has not been completed by October 13, 2026, 11:59 PM this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source will be official information from FIFA, however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Multiple European sides anchor the tight cluster of leading probabilities for the 2026 FIFA World Cup title, reflecting comparable squad depth, recent international form, and pedigree among the top contenders. Spain and France share the highest implied probabilities, buoyed by strong recent tournament showings and young attacking talent, while England, Portugal, and defending champions Argentina trail closely behind. This narrow spread highlights the competitive balance across several nations with elite rosters and favorable paths, as preparations intensify ahead of the expanded 48-team group stage opening in days.

markets.question: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if China's General Secretary of the Communist Party, Xi Jinping, is removed from power for any length of time between July 3, 2025, and December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Xi Jinping out before 2027?
events.description:

CCP General Secretary Xi Jinping will be considered removed from power if he announces his resignation from his role as General Secretary, or is otherwise dismissed, detained, disqualified, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from fulfilling his duties as General Secretary within this market's timeframe.

eventMetadata.context_description: Xi Jinping maintains firm control over the Chinese Communist Party, state institutions, and the military through loyal appointees and the absence of visible internal factions or challenges capable of forcing his removal. Recent official activities, including his 2026 New Year address, leadership in drafting the 15th Five-Year Plan, and high-level diplomatic engagements, underscore continuity into the 21st Party Congress scheduled for 2027. Analysts note that while succession discussions have begun in elite circles, no heir apparent has been elevated, and longstanding norms around age or term limits have been set aside under Xi's consolidated authority. Trader consensus at 92.8% for "No" reflects the lack of any verifiable developments or elite defections that could alter this trajectory before the end of 2026.

markets.question: Will Gavin Newsom win the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2024 election concluded, the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field remains wide open more than two years before primaries begin, producing the current fragmented trader consensus. Gavin Newsom leads at 25.1% on the strength of his California governorship, national profile, and fundraising network, while no other contender exceeds 10%. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, Jon Ossoff, and Josh Shapiro trail at single-digit shares, reflecting dispersed early positioning among governors, senators, and congressional figures. Key differentiators include state-level executive experience, donor access, and visibility in battleground states. Consolidation behind one outcome will likely hinge on 2027-2028 polling trends, primary endorsements, and performance in early caucuses or debates.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the named individual wins and accepts the 2028 nomination of the Democratic Party for U.S. president. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Democratic Party sources.

Any replacement of the democratic nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

events.title: Democratic Presidential Nominee 2028
events.description:

eventMetadata.context_description: With the 2024 election concluded, the 2028 Democratic presidential nomination field remains wide open more than two years before primaries begin, producing the current fragmented trader consensus. Gavin Newsom leads at 25.1% on the strength of his California governorship, national profile, and fundraising network, while no other contender exceeds 10%. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, Kamala Harris, Jon Ossoff, and Josh Shapiro trail at single-digit shares, reflecting dispersed early positioning among governors, senators, and congressional figures. Key differentiators include state-level executive experience, donor access, and visibility in battleground states. Consolidation behind one outcome will likely hinge on 2027-2028 polling trends, primary endorsements, and performance in early caucuses or debates.
